_meta.purpose: Machine-readable capability registry for all 6 agents. Read by: Supervisor agent at startup, Antigravity Workflow Runtime for dispatch planning, Agents CLI for execution routing, and the LLM Judge for output schema validation.

execution_environments.antigravity_app: Reads this file at startup to build the Agent Manager dispatch manifest. Each agent entry becomes a node in the Workflow Runtime graph.
execution_environments.agents_cli: Parses this file to validate --agent flag values and resolve context_key dependencies before execution.
execution_environments.llm_judge: References output_schema for each agent when scoring evaluation rubric compliance.
execution_environments.antigravity_ide: Displays agent registry in the sidebar for file-to-agent mapping.

agents.name: Intake Agent
agents.temperature_guidance: 0.6 — slightly creative to feel conversational, not robotic
agents.description: Conducts a structured 10-step conversational interview to collect a complete campaign brief from the user. The only agent that interacts directly with a human in a back-and-forth loop. All other agents are non-conversational.
agents.vibe_coding_role: Natural language IS the interface here — the user speaks plain English, the agent extracts structured data. This agent demonstrates the core vibe coding paradigm: human intent expressed in natural language, transformed into machine-readable schema.

context_writes.description: Complete structured campaign brief after user confirmation

input_spec.description: Any opening message from the user. No schema required. Agent conducts the structured interview itself.
examples: I want to set up a marketing campaign for my business
Hi, I need help with ads and SEO for my photography studio in Goa

validation_rules.page_urls: Each entry must have 'url' (valid URL) and 'type' (enum)

failure_modes.user_gives_vague_answer: Ask ONE targeted follow-up question for that specific field only
failure_modes.user_skips_page_urls: Prompt specifically: 'Please list your website pages one by one. Start with your homepage URL.'
failure_modes.max_turns_reached: Output partial IntakeBrief with null for missing fields, flag them as incomplete

test_prompts.description: Happy path — cooperative user with complete information
test_prompts.input: Hi, I want to build a campaign for my photography studio in Goa. It's called Goa Lens Studio.
test_prompts.expected_behaviour: Agent asks about address next (Group 2 of the 10-step interview), does not ask for all info at once

test_prompts.input: I do photography in India
test_prompts.expected_behaviour: Agent asks ONE specific follow-up: 'Great! Which specific cities or regions in India do you serve? For example: Goa, Mumbai, Bangalore?'

test_prompts.description: User tries to dump all info at once
test_prompts.input: My business is Goa Lens Studio at MG Road Panaji Goa, we do wedding photography and portraits, we serve Goa and Mumbai, target couples 25-35, our USPs are 10 years experience and candid style, website is goalensstudio.com, competitors are Studio XYZ, budget 30000 rupees, goal is lead gen, email is owner@goalensstudio.com
test_prompts.expected_behaviour: Agent extracts all available fields, confirms the summary, asks ONLY for the missing page URLs list before outputting IntakeBrief

agents.name: Supervisor Agent
agents.temperature_guidance: 0.1 — deterministic; validation and dispatch must be consistent
agents.description: The orchestration hub. Validates the IntakeBrief, decomposes the campaign goal into parallel tasks, dispatches subagents via A2A, merges outputs into bundles, enforces HITL checkpoints, runs the cross-channel consistency check, and manages error recovery.
agents.vibe_coding_role: The supervisor's behaviour is entirely defined by its natural language persona file (agents/supervisor.md). The Workflow Runtime reads the persona, builds the dispatch logic, and manages graph traversal. No Python orchestration code needed.

context_writes.description: Decomposed task list with per-agent field assignments
Merged output of all 3 strategy agents + consistency check result
Merged output of copywriter + visual agents

failure_modes.agent_timeout: Flag timeout in bundle, continue with partial bundle, notify user at next HITL
failure_modes.schema_validation_fail: Return targeted clarification to user for specific invalid fields
failure_modes.consistency_check_fail: Add warning to StrategyBundle, surface at HITL #1 with specific recommendation

test_prompts.input: { complete IntakeBrief JSON from intake agent }
test_prompts.expected_output: task_manifest with validation_status: valid, 3 parallel_tasks dispatched

test_prompts.input: { IntakeBrief with empty services array and missing user_email }
{ IntakeBrief where SEO targets 'commercial photographer Mumbai' but Meta targets 'wedding audience Goa' }
test_prompts.expected_output: consistency_check.consistent: false, misalignments: ['SEO location (Mumbai) conflicts with Meta audience location (Goa)'], recommendation: 'Align both to Goa + Mumbai dual-market strategy'

agents.name: SEO / AEO / GEO Strategy Agent
agents.description: Produces three interconnected outputs: (1) 30-keyword research report with AEO/GEO potential scoring, (2) JSON-LD schema blocks for every page URL submitted, (3) internal linking cluster map with pillar/satellite structure and content gap analysis.
agents.vibe_coding_role: Agent's entire behaviour specified in agents/seo_agent.md. Antigravity resolves the grounding tool call automatically from mcp_config.json. No Python tool-calling code required.
context_reads: intake.brief.v1 (filtered: business_name, services, service_areas, page_urls, competitors, target_customer)

context_writes.description: Full SEO strategy: keyword report + schemas per URL + internal linking map

self_validation.schema_check: After generating each JSON-LD block, verify @context is present, @type is valid Schema.org type, required fields for that type are non-null
self_validation.on_failure: Regenerate the specific failing schema block once with validation error appended to prompt

test_prompts.input: { filtered IntakeBrief: business_name: Goa Lens Studio, services: [wedding photography, portrait, commercial], service_areas: [Goa, Mumbai], page_urls: [{url: goalensstudio.com, type: homepage}, {url: goalensstudio.com/services, type: service}] }
test_prompts.expected_output: 30 keywords with intent labels, 2 URL schema blocks (LocalBusiness+Org+WebSite+SiteLinksSearchBox for homepage; Service+LocalBusiness+FAQPage+BreadcrumbList for service page), 1 internal linking cluster

test_prompts.input: { page_url: { url: goalensstudio.com/portfolio, type: portfolio } }
test_prompts.expected_output: JSON-LD blocks: ImageGallery, ImageObject, CreativeWork, Photograph — all with real business data populated

test_prompts.description: AEO gap check for photography keywords
test_prompts.input: { keyword: 'best wedding photographer Goa', target_business: Goa Lens Studio }
test_prompts.expected_output: aeo_potential: high, content_gaps includes 'FAQ page answering top 10 wedding photography questions', aeo_opportunities includes question-format keywords

agents.name: Google Ads Strategy Agent
agents.temperature_guidance: 0.2 — precise; campaign structure must be actionable and specific
agents.description: Builds a complete Google Ads campaign structure: ad groups with tightly themed keyword lists, match type recommendations, negative keyword lists, CPC estimates in INR, bid strategy, location targeting, ad schedule, and audience layering suggestions.
context_reads: intake.brief.v1 (filtered: business_name, services, service_areas, target_customer, monthly_budget, campaign_goal, competitors)

context_writes.description: Complete campaign structure with all ad groups, keywords, and bid strategy

test_prompts.input: { business: Goa Lens Studio, services: [wedding photography, portraits, commercial], service_areas: [Goa, Mumbai], target_customer: couples 24-35 planning destination weddings, budget: ₹30000, goal: lead_generation }
test_prompts.expected_output: 3-4 ad groups (Wedding Goa, Destination Wedding, Portrait Sessions, Commercial), daily budget ₹1000, bid strategy: Maximize Conversions, negative keywords include [free, DIY, course, stock photo]

test_prompts.input: { budget: ₹5000, campaign_goal: lead_generation }
test_prompts.expected_output: Recommendation to use Search only (not PMax), 1-2 ad groups maximum, note about budget constraints limiting scale

test_prompts.input: { service_areas: [Goa, Mumbai, Bangalore, Chennai], budget: ₹60000 }
test_prompts.expected_output: Location-specific ad groups or location bid adjustments, recommendation to separate campaigns by city for budget control

agents.name: Meta Ads Strategy Agent
agents.temperature_guidance: 0.3 — creative enough for audience ideation, precise enough for targeting specs
agents.description: Designs a complete Meta Ads audience funnel across three stages (TOFU/MOFU/BOFU) with specific interest categories, behaviour targets, lookalike seeds, retargeting windows, ad format recommendations per stage, budget split, and exclusion audiences.
context_reads: intake.brief.v1 (filtered: target_customer, service_areas, monthly_budget, unique_value_props, campaign_goal, services)

context_writes.description: Full Meta audience funnel strategy with TOFU/MOFU/BOFU structure

test_prompts.input: { target_customer: couples 24-35 planning destination weddings in Goa, budget: ₹30000, unique_value_props: [candid style, drone shots, 72hr preview], campaign_goal: lead_generation }
test_prompts.expected_output: TOFU interests: [destination weddings, bridal fashion, honeymoon travel], MOFU: video viewers 50%+ + engaged with page, BOFU: website visitors 30 days + lead form openers. Budget split 40/35/25. Frequency cap 3/week TOFU.

test_prompts.description: Verify TOFU/MOFU/BOFU are genuinely distinct audiences (not overlapping)
test_prompts.input: { same as TP-META-1 }
test_prompts.expected_output: Exclusion: BOFU audience excluded from TOFU. MOFU excludes BOFU. Each stage has distinct targeting mechanism: interest vs. engagement vs. retargeting. Creative direction clearly differs per stage.

test_prompts.input: { campaign_goal: brand_awareness, budget: ₹20000 }
test_prompts.expected_output: Heavier TOFU allocation (60%), Reels-first format, no lead form ad format recommended, pixel events focus on video views and profile visits, not form completions

agents.name: Ad Copywriter Agent
agents.temperature_guidance: Writer call: 0.7 (creative, varied). Judge call: 0.1 (consistent scoring).
agents.description: A two-pass agent. Pass 1 (Writer): generates Google RSA copy (15 headlines + 4 descriptions per ad group) and Meta copy for all 3 funnel stages with diverse psychological angles. Pass 2 (Judge): a second Gemini call scores every headline on clarity, hook, and relevance — only top-scored variants surface to the human at HITL #2.
agents.vibe_coding_role: The two-LLM-call pattern (writer + judge) demonstrates LLM-as-Judge evaluation in production. The judge IS an evaluation step embedded inside an agent — a key course concept.
context_reads: strategy.bundle.v1 (reads: gads.strategy.v1 for ad group themes, meta.strategy.v1 for funnel stage creative directions)

context_writes.description: All ad copy variants with LLM judge scores attached

self_validation.char_count_enforcement: Before outputting any headline, count characters. If count > 30, shorten and recount. Do NOT output headlines over limit.
self_validation.judge_consistency: Judge model temperature is 0.1 — run judge twice if scores differ by more than 2 points on any headline
self_validation.meta_tofu_bofu_distinction: Verify: TOFU primary_text does NOT mention price or offer. BOFU primary_text MUST contain a specific offer or next step.

test_prompts.description: RSA headline generation for wedding photography ad group
test_prompts.input: { ad_group: Wedding Photography Goa, keywords: [wedding photographer goa, candid wedding photography goa], UVPs: [10 years experience, candid style, drone shots, 72hr preview], target: couples 24-35 }
test_prompts.expected_output: 15 headlines all <= 30 chars, diverse angles present (not all 'feature'), judge scores attached, recommended: true on top 10

test_prompts.input: { requested headline: 'Award-Winning Candid Wedding Photography Goa — Book Now' }
test_prompts.expected_output: Agent self-corrects: original is 53 chars. Outputs shortened version <= 30 chars, e.g. 'Award-Winning Wedding Photos' (29 chars), notes the self-correction in output

test_prompts.description: Meta TOFU vs BOFU distinction check
test_prompts.input: { services: wedding photography, UVPs: [candid style, drone shots], TOFU_direction: aspirational, BOFU_direction: direct offer }
test_prompts.expected_output: TOFU primary_text: aspirational, no price mention, no 'book now'. BOFU primary_text: specific offer ('Book your consultation — limited slots for December weddings'), clear CTA.

agents.name: Visual Creative Agent
agents.temperature_guidance: Style extraction: 0.3 (precise visual analysis). Prompt generation: 0.8 (creative, imaginative).
agents.description: A multimodal agent. Phase 1: Uses Gemini Vision to analyse uploaded portfolio photos and extract a structured visual style profile. Phase 2: Uses the style profile to write Imagen 3 prompts for 3 ad formats (1:1, 4:5, 9:16) and 2 Reel scripts (15s + 30s) — all aesthetically matched to the photographer's existing style.
context_reads: meta.strategy.v1 (reads: ad_formats per funnel stage for format alignment)

context_writes.description: Style profile + Imagen prompts + Reel scripts

ad_image_concepts.imagen_prompt: string (detailed, style-matched, no text in image)

test_prompts.description: Style extraction from uploaded portfolio images
test_prompts.input: { portfolio_images: [path/to/wedding1.jpg, path/to/portrait1.jpg, path/to/commercial1.jpg], business: Goa Lens Studio }
test_prompts.expected_output: style_profile.color_palette populated with real observed values, style_summary: 2-3 sentences accurately describing the photographer's aesthetic, imagen_style_tags: [cinematic, warm tones, natural light, shallow depth of field, golden hour] (example — based on actual images)

test_prompts.description: Imagen prompt generation for 9x16 Story format
test_prompts.input: { style_profile: [from TP-VIS-1], target: couples 24-35, service: wedding photography, format: 9x16 }
test_prompts.expected_output: imagen_prompt contains: subject description, lighting direction, color grade instruction, composition (rule of thirds or centre), mood, lens/depth-of-field language, explicit 'no text in image' instruction

test_prompts.description: 15-second Reel script with pattern interrupt hook
test_prompts.input: { service: wedding photography Goa, target: engaged couples, style: warm, candid }
test_prompts.expected_output: hook at 0-2s: visually surprising shot (not a standard wedding couple pose), 4-5 scene breakdown, each scene <= 4 seconds, CTA in final 2s: 'Link in bio — your Goa wedding story starts here'

_delivery_phase.note: The delivery phase is not an agent — it is a MCP pipeline triggered by the Supervisor after HITL #3 approval.

steps.content: delivery email with Drive folder link
